---
title: "Configuring A Content Federated Connector"
---

Content Federated connectors are configured as part of a project within the Studio Modeler application.

The steps in this topic only cover processes specific to Content Federated connectors within a project, and the steps assume that you are familiar with creating projects in the Studio Modeler application. For more information on using the Studio Modeler application, see Projects in the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation.
To configure a Content Federated connector in a project:

1.  Sign in to the Studio Modeler application and open the project that you would like to include a Content Federated connector.
2.  Open the process that you would like to include a Content Federated connector.
3.  In the Tool palette, click the Connectors button (![Connectors button](data:image/gif;base64,)). A selection of available connector types is displayed.
4.  Click the Create Content Federated connector button (![Create Content Federated connector button](data:image/gif;base64,)).
5.  At the prompt, choose one of the following options:
- If this is the first time using the Content Federated connector within the project, click Create a new instance and give it a name.
- If the connector has already been used within the project and the tasks will share the same configuration patterns, select an existing instance.
6.  Drag the connector onto the diagram canvas. When dropped, the connector is selected and its properties are displayed in the Properties pane on the right.
7.  Fill in the properties in the Properties pane:
| Property | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ID | Required | Identifier of the connector. It is given automatically by the Modeler application. |
| Name | Optional | Optional name of the connector. When entered, it is displayed in the connector rectangle within the process diagram. |
| Documentation | Optional | Description of the connector that can be useful for people working on the process. |
| Asynchronous Continuation | Optional | An option to execute the event in the background, using a separate transaction that runs concurrently to other transactions. |
| Multi Instance Type | Required | Option allowing to repeat the element within the process. The default setting is None. |
| Implementation [Connector name] | Required | Name of the instance selected or given when creating the connector. |
| Action | Required | Action that the connector will perform in the process. For more information on available actions, see Available Actions. |

Note: Connectors are treated as repository administrators by default, which means they can access all files and folders in the repository.
8.  Once the action is selected, from the Mapping type drop-down menu, select a mapping type.
9.  Depending on your method of user configuration for the Content Federated connector, do one of the following. For information on how to determine which configuration to use, see Methods of User Configuration.
- If the Content Federated connector is connecting through a service user (SU), skip to step 13.
- If the Content Federated connector is connecting on behalf of (OBO) an end user, define the value for the task parameter.
1.  Under Input mapping, click the Edit icon in the task parameter entry. The Edit variable mapping dialog box is displayed.
2.  Click the Value tab.
3.  In the Task with assigned form drop-down menu, select the preceding task that contains the action being requested by the user. This allows the task to run on behalf of the end user that performed the action. For example, if the preceding task is Select Type, the Content Federated connector requires that the task input parameter is set to Select Type.
4.  Click Update.
10.  Access the Content Federated connector's configuration parameters by doing one of the following:
- Click the tab labeled [Connector name].
- From the left sidebar, click Connectors > [Connector name].
The Connector Details page for the Content Federated connector is displayed.
11.  Under Configuration parameters, enter values for the parameters in the Value field below the descriptions. The following table lists the configuration parameters and what value to put in their Value field:

| Parameter Name | Value to Input |
| --- | --- |
| CONNECTOR_CONNECTION_IDENTIFIER | Input the Connection ID of the CFS connection being used for the connector. This value may be found in the Connection ID field in the Connection details dialog box in the Content Federation Admin Portal. See Checking CFS Connections. |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_ID | Input the Application ID. The Application ID is created when registering the CFS Consumer API, and it is available in the Content Innovation Cloud (CIC) Administration Portal under External Applications. For more information, see Registering the CFS Consumer API in the Content Innovation Cloud. |
| CONNECTOR_EXTERNAL_APPLICATION_SECRET | Input the application Secret. This Secret value is created when registering the CFS Consumer API in the Content Innovation Cloud (CIC) Administration Portal. For more information, see Registering the CFS Consumer API in the Content Innovation Cloud. |

12.  To save the connector, click the Save connector button (![Save connector button](data:image/gif;base64,)) below the tab list.
13.  To save the process and the connector's place in the process:
1.  Select the process by doing one of the following:
- Click the tab labeled [Process name], where [Process Name] is the process selected in step 2.
- From the left sidebar, click Processes > [Process Name], where [Process Name] is the process selected in step 2.
2.  Click the Save process button (![Save process button](data:image/gif;base64,)) below the tab list.

If the Content Federated connector is created in a project but not used in any process, it is not deployed into the file application. You need to create a process, include the project with the Content Federated connector, and release the process before it can be deployed. For more information about working with processes in Automate, see Processes in the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation.
